title: net-triage output data
description: Shape of the ``data`` field on a ``net-triage`` envelope.

control.description: The one control-plane HTTPS check (shared by every host verdict).

hosts.description: Per-host differential: every configured cluster host + any caller host.
hosts.title: Hosts

all_reachable.description: True when every triaged host's verdict is 'reachable'.
all_reachable.title: All Reachable

summary.description: One-line human digest: each host's verdict, or 'no hosts to triage'.
summary.title: Summary

active_env_overrides.description: Every framework-relevant HPC_* environment variable currently exported in THIS process's environment, verbatim. Pure disclosure — triage never judges the values (run-12 finding 24 addendum, B15). A stray transport override like HPC_SSH_ENGINE='asyncssh' or HPC_SSH_CIRCUIT_OVERRIDE silently reroutes/short-circuits the very SSH this differential diagnoses, so the env that shaped the verdict is echoed alongside it: an override that shows up here unexpectedly IS the finding. Empty when no HPC_* variable is set.
active_env_overrides.title: Active Env Overrides

BreakerState.description:
One host's SSH circuit-breaker state, read from its durable state file.

Read-only: triage NEVER writes breaker state, never claims the half-open
probe slot, and never counts toward the failure ledger. ``missing`` means
no state file exists (a healthy host that never failed) and is treated as
closed everywhere — fail-open, same posture as the breaker itself.

state.description: EFFECTIVE breaker state at read time: 'open' fails SSH fast (ban-risk protection, still cooling), 'half_open_eligible' means the cooldown has lapsed and the next real SSH connection will run the single half-open probe (success closes the circuit, failure re-opens it with a doubled cooldown) — nothing fails fast anymore, 'closed' is healthy, 'missing' means no state file (never failed).
state.title: State

consecutive_failures.description: Consecutive connection-level failures recorded for this host.
consecutive_failures.title: Consecutive Failures

cooldown_until.description: When the cooldown ends/ended and the half-open probe becomes eligible (ISO-8601 UTC). Null unless state is 'open' or 'half_open_eligible' (where it is the already-past lapse time).
cooldown_until.title: Cooldown Until

last_failure_at.description: When the last connection-level failure was recorded (ISO-8601 UTC), or null.
last_failure_at.title: Last Failure At

last_failure_detail.description: The matched failure marker + stderr snippet of the last failure, or null.
last_failure_detail.title: Last Failure Detail

BreakerState.title: net-triage breaker state

ControlPlaneCheck.description:
The one bounded HTTPS probe that separates 'my network is down' from
'the cluster is unreachable' — run once per invocation, not per host.

https_ok.description: True when the control endpoint answered over HTTPS (any HTTP status).
https_ok.title: Https Ok

detail.description: 'HTTP <status>' on success; the exception class + message on failure.
detail.title: Detail

ControlPlaneCheck.title: net-triage control-plane check

HostTriage.description: The full connectivity differential for one host, with a verdict.

host.description: The host triaged (breaker key: bare hostname, no user@).
host.title: Host

cluster.description: The clusters.yaml entry this host came from, or null for a caller host.
cluster.title: Cluster

breaker.description: Circuit-breaker state read from the state file.

dns_ok.description: Whether the hostname resolved (bounded). Null when not attempted.
dns_ok.title: Dns Ok

tcp_ok.description: Whether ONE bounded TCP connect to host:22 succeeded. Null when the probe was SKIPPED — always skipped while the breaker is open and still cooling (the half-open probe slot belongs to the breaker, never to triage) and when DNS already failed. A half_open_eligible breaker DOES get the probe: it is evidence only, never a circuit transition (triage never claims the probe slot).
tcp_ok.title: Tcp Ok

tcp_detail.description: Connect detail, error, or the reason the probe was skipped.
tcp_detail.title: Tcp Detail

verdict.description: The differential's answer: 'reachable' (host:22 accepts TCP); 'breaker_open_cooling' (SSH is failing fast by design — wait or override); 'host_unreachable_network_ok' (control passes, host doesn't — cluster-side outage or source-IP filter); 'local_network_down' (the control probe failed — fix THIS machine's network/VPN first); 'dns_failure' (hostname didn't resolve).
verdict.title: Verdict

remediation.description: What to do about the verdict — deterministic text, one per verdict arm.
remediation.title: Remediation

HostTriage.title: net-triage per-host result
